executionTimeline.description: The reduce method is called. The accumulator 'acc' is initialized to the second argument: 0.
The engine picks the first element of the array (10). The reducer function is called with acc=0 and curr=10.
The result of the first iteration (10) becomes the 'acc' for the next iteration.
The engine picks the second element (20). The reducer function is called with acc=10 and curr=20.
The engine picks the final element (30). The reducer function is called with acc=30 and curr=30.
No more elements left. The final value of the accumulator (60) is returned as the result of the reduce call.

theFix: If you omit the initial value, the first element of the array is used as the accumulator and the loop starts from the second element. Always provide an initial value (like 0 for sums or {} for objects) to prevent errors on empty arrays.
